cataloging_subject.label: Nachträge zu den Legenden (CME00063 selected serial blocks)
cataloging_subject.scope_note: This record describes the University of Michigan/CME selection assembled from five independently published Carl Horstmann installments in Archiv volumes 62, 68, 74, 79, and 80. It is not one medieval work, one codex, or the complete serialized Nachträge sequence: the encoded portion of volume 68 omits an earlier continuation printed in that volume. Five serial parent blocks and twenty-seven leaf-level edited witness streams are therefore cataloged explicitly.

catalog_summary.title: Nachträge zu den Legenden
catalog_summary.author: Anonymous medieval authors; John Lydgate (calendar attributed)
catalog_summary.date: late thirteenth to fifteenth century texts; selected editions published 1879–1888
catalog_summary.region: England and Scotland, with part-specific dialect regions

notes: A supplied English gloss, not a title printed by Horstmann.

agents.name: Anonymous medieval authors
notes: A cataloging collective for separately transmitted anonymous components; it does not imply common authorship.

scope.description: All medieval components except the three calendar witness streams attributed to John Lydgate.

notes: The Alexius section is labeled by Horstmann as from 'Barbour's' legendary, but modern cataloging should not convert that collection label into secure authorship by John Barbour.

scope.description: The three separately printed witness versions of A Kalendare.

notes: The current British Library record describes the Harley copy as attributed to Lydgate; Horstmann presents the attribution more directly.

scope.description: All five encoded journal installments and their apparatus.

date_statements.display: chiefly late thirteenth through fifteenth century; part-specific dates differ

scope.description: Broad cataloging envelope for the medieval texts, not a single composition date.

notes: Witness production, composition, revision, and the nineteenth-century publication dates are separate layers.

scope.description: Publication dates of the five selected Horstmann installments.

notes: Horstmann labels the edited witness language Altschottisch; this is a dialect assertion about that textual witness, not the codex's present location.
Horstmann describes the Rawlinson witness as in the dialect of Oxford; this does not establish the calendar's original place of composition.

scope.description: Most medieval edited texts and all three calendar streams.

form_statements.description: A mixed-form edited selection, not an intrinsically mixed single medieval work.

scope.description: Saints' lives and feast legends across several blocks.
Visions, death poems, Marian lament, calendars, and salvation narratives.
Exempla and closely related moral tales.

subject_statements.label: Death, hell, judgment, and the afterlife
Parallel manuscript versions and textual transmission

summaries.value: A selective digital compilation of five Carl Horstmann journal installments presenting saints' lives, biblical and apocryphal narratives, moral and devotional poems, three versions of Robert of Sicily, two Long Charter versions, two English and two Latin Adam traditions, and three witness versions of an attributed Lydgate calendar. The texts come from more than twenty physical manuscripts and several intermediary scholarly transcripts.

The recurring journal title does not establish one medieval work. The first XML DIV1 joins the 1879 volume-62 installment to only the final 1882 volume-68 installment; the earlier continuation on volume-68 p. 52 is not encoded. The twenty-seven counted leaf streams exclude Add MS 22283 variant apparatus while retaining independently printed witness versions.

extent.description: Cataloger count of twenty-seven independently printed witness streams nested under five serial blocks. The XML contains 4 DIV1, 26 DIV2, and 30 DIV3 elements (60 structural divisions), 798 LG elements, and 9,849 L elements. These are encoding metrics: variant apparatus, parallel-column serialization, prose tables, and the three interleaved calendar streams mean raw XML totals are not canonical line or stanza counts for each intellectual work.

content_parts.label: Nachträge zu den Legenden, Archiv 62 (1879)
content_parts.summary: First selected Horstmann installment: Alexius, Eleven Pains, an incomplete Cotton Susanna, Erasmus, and three complete Robert of Sicily witness versions.

content_parts.label: Alexius from the Scottish Legendary
content_parts.summary: A Scots verse life of Saint Alexius, printed from CUL MS Gg.2.6.

locators.value: 163r–169r; last leaf shared with next item

content_parts.label: Les ounsse peines de enfer / The Eleven Pains of Hell
content_parts.summary: A sixteen-line Anglo-Norman opening followed by a Middle English vision of hell, printed from MS Digby 86.

content_parts.label: The Pistill of Susan (Cotton Caligula A II version)
content_parts.summary: An imperfect alliterative Susanna version whose opening 104 lines are lost; Add MS 22283 variants are apparatus rather than a separately counted stream.

content_parts.label: Saint Erasmus
content_parts.summary: The older complete Erasmus verse witness printed from CUL MS Dd.1.1, with unresolved modern/legacy foliation crosswalks.

locators.value: scholarly ff. 295r–296v; 1856 catalog manuscript pp. 537–540; alternate handlist ff. 258r–259v

content_parts.label: Robert of Sicily (CUL MS Ii.4.9 version)
content_parts.summary: The first of three complete witness versions printed separately by Horstmann.

content_parts.label: Robert of Sicily (Caius MS 174/95 version)
content_parts.summary: The second complete witness version, from Gonville and Caius College MS 174/95.

content_parts.label: Robert of Sicily (CUL MS Ff.2.38 version)
content_parts.summary: The third complete witness version; Horstmann's legacy fol. 93ff. conflicts with the modern ff. 254r–257v locus.

content_parts.label: Nachträge zu den Legenden (Schluß), Archiv 68 (1882)
content_parts.summary: The encoded final installment from volume 68, containing the Sion witness of the Gospel of Nicodemus; an earlier continuation in the same volume is not encoded.

content_parts.label: Gospel of Nicodemus (Sion version)
content_parts.summary: A northern Middle English verse Gospel of Nicodemus printed from former Sion College MS L40.2/E25, now at Lambeth Palace Library.

content_parts.label: Nachträge zu den Legenden, Archiv 74 (1885)
content_parts.summary: A Childhood of Jesus, a Susanna witness, and two prose Life of Adam versions, including sections mediated by transcripts supplied to Horstmann.

content_parts.label: Childhood of Jesus
content_parts.summary: A northern verse childhood/apocryphal narrative printed from Kölbing's transcript of BL Add MS 31042.

locators.value: 163v–168v; ending inferred from next item at f. 169

content_parts.label: Susanna (Huntington HM 114 version)
content_parts.summary: A separately printed Susanna witness from former Cheltenham/Phillipps 8252, mediated by Kölbing's transcript.

content_parts.label: Life of Adam (Bodley 596 version)
content_parts.summary: The first prose Adam version, printed through a Furnivall transcript; its terminal folio requires reconciliation.

locators.value: catalog ff. 1r–11v; XML milestones continue to f. 12

content_parts.label: Life of Adam and Eve (Harley 4775 Gilte Legende version)
content_parts.summary: The second prose version, incorporated into a Gilte Legende manuscript.

locators.value: scholarly ff. 258v–264r; cataloged Gilte Legende ends f. 262v

content_parts.label: Nachträge zu den Legenden, Archiv 79 (1887)
content_parts.summary: Twelve edited streams: saints' lives, moral tales, two parallel Long Charter versions, death and feast poems, an Old Testament episode, a Marian lament, and two Latin Adam/cross texts.

content_parts.label: Seint Margarete þe holy virgine
content_parts.summary: A younger South English Legendary life of Saint Margaret printed from MS Bodley 779.

content_parts.label: Punishment of Adultery
content_parts.summary: A short moral tale printed from MS Rawlinson poetry 118.

locators.value: 113v–116r; abbreviated catalog appears to omit leading 11

content_parts.label: A Tale of an Incestuous Daughter
content_parts.summary: A short moral tale following the adultery poem in MS Rawlinson poetry 118.

content_parts.label: The Long Charter / Testamentum Christi (Vernon version)
content_parts.summary: The Vernon text printed in parallel columns with the Harley version.

content_parts.label: The Long Charter / Testamentum Christi (Harley 2382 version)
content_parts.summary: The Harley text printed in parallel columns with the Vernon version.

content_parts.label: The Three Messengers of Death
content_parts.summary: A short death poem from the Vernon Manuscript.

content_parts.label: The Founding of the Feasts of All Saints and All Souls
content_parts.summary: A corruptly transmitted feast legend printed from MS Ashmole 61.

content_parts.label: Romance of Christ's Resurrection
content_parts.summary: A unique, disordered, revised, and lacunose verse witness printed from MS Ashmole 61.

content_parts.label: De matre et VII pueris
content_parts.summary: The mother and seven sons episode at the end of the Middle English Metrical Paraphrase of the Old Testament, printed from Longleat MS 257 through a friend's transcript.

content_parts.label: Lamentation of Our Lady
content_parts.summary: A Middle English prose Marian lament from MS Bodley 596.

content_parts.label: Vita prothoplasti Adae
content_parts.summary: A Latin prose Life of Adam printed directly from Queen's College Oxford MS 213; Horstmann's proposed ancestry for the English versions remains a historical editorial claim.

content_parts.label: De ligno sanctae crucis
content_parts.summary: A Latin prose legend of the wood of the Cross printed in sequence with the Queen's MS 213 Adam text; the exact manuscript endpoint remains to be confirmed.

content_parts.label: Nachträge zu den Legenden, Archiv 80 (1888)
content_parts.summary: Three separately serialized manuscript versions of the attributed Lydgate verse calendar, physically interleaved in the printed article.

content_parts.label: A Kalendare (Harley 4011 version)
content_parts.summary: The Harley reworking, lacking January and February because its opening leaf is missing.

content_parts.label: A Kalendare (Douce 322 version)
content_parts.summary: Horstmann's preferred of the three surviving witness texts, serialized as January–February and March–December streams around the other versions.

content_parts.label: A Kalendare (Rawlinson B. 408 version)
content_parts.summary: The Oxford-dialect witness, serialized separately from the Douce text despite sharing the article's odd printed pages.

related_works.title: Legenda aurea / The Golden Legend
notes: Horstmann invokes the Legenda aurea for Alexius and the Gilte Legende Adam tradition; no single physical Latin exemplar is established for the entire selection.

related_works.title: Daniel 13 / the story of Susanna
notes: Source tradition for the two independently printed Middle English Susanna witnesses.

related_works.title: Middle English Metrical Paraphrase of the Old Testament
notes: De matre et VII pueris is the final episode, not an independent codex item.

related_works.title: Vita Adae et Evae traditions
notes: The two English prose versions and two Latin texts are related but should not be collapsed into one directly evidenced line of descent.

evidence.summary: Identifies CME00063 and preserves the five selected serial blocks, source headings, texts, witness labels, page and folio milestones, editorial notes, languages, and apparatus.

evidence.locator: ETS/EEBO/TEXT element counts and division tree
evidence.summary: Counts 4 DIV1, 26 DIV2, 30 DIV3, 798 LG, 9,849 L, 62 P, 193 PB, 2,121 MILESTONE, 556 NOTE, 9,788 italic HI, 12 TABLE, 365 ROW, and 1,825 CELL elements; confirms repeated physical pages in parallel and serialized calendar streams.

evidence.citation: Linked lineage evidence for University of Michigan CME00063.
evidence.summary: Supports the public item title, editor attribution, publication range, and encoding identity.

evidence.citation: Linked lineage evidence from complete scans of Archiv volumes 62, 68, 74, 79, and 80.
evidence.summary: Supports the five independent article identities, exact printed page ranges, source headings, physical parallel-column layout, serial boundary, and publication facts.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, Robert of Cisyle.
evidence.summary: Supports romance classification, approximate late-fourteenth-century and South-East Midlands composition, the three Horstmann witnesses, and modern Ff.2.38 and Ii.4.9 loci.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, The Three Messengers of Death.
evidence.summary: Supports title, West Midlands textual localization, Vernon/Simeon transmission, and modern witness context.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, Middle English Metrical Paraphrase of the Old Testament.
evidence.summary: Identifies De matre et VII pueris as the closing episode of the larger paraphrase, supports the c. 1400–1410 northern composition, and distinguishes Longleat MS 257 from an independent work witness.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 4011.
evidence.summary: Catalogs A Kalendare as attributed to John Lydgate and supplies the Harley witness locus and current unavailable-image status.

evidence.citation: Linked lineage evidence for MS Bodley 779 and its complete 672-canvas surrogate.
evidence.summary: Supports the current shelfmark, second-quarter fifteenth-century witness, complete object, and Margaret mapping at ff. 204v–208r.

evidence.citation: Linked lineage evidence for Queen's College Oxford MS 213.
evidence.summary: Supports the 1449 codex, Vita prothoplasti Adae at ff. 1r–7v, and the following item beginning f. 8.

open_questions.question: Should future cataloging expose each of the twenty-seven witness streams as a first-class child work record in addition to this edited-selection record?
next_steps: Assign stable child-work identifiers without implying that every witness version is an independent medieval composition.
Preserve the five serial blocks as publication containers and the physical witnesses as lineage entities.

open_questions.question: What are the best current composition dates and dialect localizations for every separately transmitted component?
next_steps: Research each component against current critical editions and linguistic scholarship.
Do not infer composition from a witness date or repository location.

open_questions.question: Does Queen's College MS 213 represent the source tradition behind either English Life of Adam version, as Horstmann suggests?
next_steps: Compare the English texts with modern Vita Adae scholarship and the Queen's witness.
Retain the confirmed relation only for Horstmann's separately printed Latin section until textual descent is demonstrated.

notes: The metadata intentionally avoids assigning one medieval author, date, region, or form to all components.
The twenty-seven item count treats separately printed complete or partial witness texts as leaf streams; it excludes Add MS 22283 variant apparatus and contextual comparison witnesses.

The XML's 193 PB elements exceed the number of physical article pages represented because Archiv 79 prints Long Charter witnesses in parallel and Archiv 80 interleaves three calendar versions; the encoding serializes those streams.
Horstmann's article in Archiv 80 begins on printed p. 114, despite some later summaries beginning it at p. 115.
